what_it_does: Defines a single exported string constant that acts as a marker to identify summarizer agent conversations so they can be excluded from indexing.
observations: File contains one exported constant SUMMARIZER_CONTEXT_MARKER
The marker is a full English sentence used as a sentinel string
A JSDoc block explains the marker's role in excluding agent conversations from indexing
The constant is implicitly typed as string
standouts: The marker text is a human-readable sentence rather than a short token or UUID
It is used as a content-based filter signal
conventions: Uses a JSDoc block comment for documentation
Uses UPPER_SNAKE_CASE for the constant name
concerns: A human-readable marker string could be accidentally typed or matched by user content
Changing the marker text requires updating any prompts that emit it
notes: Single sentinel constant for filtering summarizer-generated context from search indexes.
questions: Where exactly is this marker injected into prompts?
How is the matching performed (exact, substring, case-sensitive)?
What happens if the marker text appears in non-agent content?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it defines a marker string for excluding summarizer conversations from indexing.
intention_signals: Provide a stable, recognizable substring that summarizer prompts include and the indexing pipeline can detect to skip those conversations.
contribution: Supplies a named, documented sentinel value that decouples the marker text from the code that matches against it, making the filter criterion explicit and maintainable.
summary_for_embedding: This file exports a single string constant used as a marker to identify summarizer agent conversations for exclusion from indexing.
intention_for_embedding: It was intended to create a detectable sentinel that summarizer prompts include so the recollection system can filter out agent-generated summaries from search results.
